summary: Joe Reis argues that AI adoption is now mandatory for career survival, dividing people into 'Builders' who experiment with AI and 'Holdouts' who resist it. He claims his AI-native workflow compresses weeks of work into days and warns that those who remain spectators will be 'crushed beyond recognition' by the coming AI wave.
key_points: People divide into Builders (AI adopters) and Holdouts (AI denialists)
AI-native workflows can compress weeks of work into days
Both technical and non-technical teams are seeing outsized results from AI adoption
There's no going back to pre-genAI by choice
Using AI tools like Claude, GPT, and Gemini is like having a bench of co-founders
money_quotes: Scared money don't make money. The biggest tsunami you've ever imagined is approaching your business, your careers, and you personally.
Often, they're repeating the same year of experience twenty times and calling it twenty years.
With the big AIs (hi Claude, GPT, Gemini), it feels like having a bench of co-founders. We strategize, build, and execute with serious velocity.
If you remain a spectator, the wave won't slow down for you. You're going to get crushed beyond recognition.
I'm betting on the builders. Everyone else is too late and f'd.
key_insight: AI adoption is no longer optional—those who refuse to integrate AI into their workflows risk becoming professionally obsolete as the technology creates an insurmountable productivity gap between adopters and holdouts.
